vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

generated_parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine

verify.value: All required features and interfaces are present.
verify.rule: Each required evidence_feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets are present in part_tree and geometry_ops.
verify.rule: P0 internal_feature_targets must appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.

verify.value: All interfaces have reciprocal naming and joint families.
verify.rule: Name exact neighboring subsystem labels when they mate, and choose connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature.

verify.value: Visible locking/preload evidence for critical joints.
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

verify.value: Fixture_points, thermal_zones, pressure_faces are defined.
verify.rule: verify must include a boundary_conditions object with fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high heat dissipation from avionics bay.
risk.mit: Implement heat sinks and thermal management systems.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under extreme load conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis and reinforce critical areas.

risk.desc: Possible misalignment during assembly due to tolerance stack-up.
risk.mit: Improve manufacturing precision and add alignment aids.

risk.desc: Risk of water ingress through belly camera window.
risk.mit: Enhance sealing and waterproofing measures.

risk.desc: Potential electrical interference from avionics bay.
risk.mit: Implement shielding and grounding solutions.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP Box Beam Center Fuselage, Avionics Bay, Wing Spar Socket, Front GPS Boom Mount, Belly Camera Window

physics_paths.description: Transfers nacelle loads from wing spar socket to fuselage spine
Passes wing spar through fuselage spine
Dissipates heat from avionics bay to fuselage spine
Allows GPS boom to pivot relative to fuselage spine
Maintains pressure integrity around belly camera window
Routes electrical and data connections from avionics bay to fuselage spine
Provides access for maintenance and service of avionics bay

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Install wing spar socket before avionics bay
Install avionics bay after fuselage spine
Install GPS boom mount after avionics bay

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: H7/h6 fit class for bolted interfaces, LC2 for snap fit interfaces
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for overhangs, orientation for minimal support
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for surface finish, CMM for dimensional accuracy

verification_focus.description: Validate load transfer from wing spar socket to fuselage spine
Ensure wing spar can pass through fuselage spine
Verify heat dissipation from avionics bay to fuselage spine
Confirm GPS boom can pivot relative to fuselage spine
Ensure pressure integrity around belly camera window
Check electrical and data connections from avionics bay to fuselage spine
Ensure access for maintenance and service of avionics bay

generated_parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)

verify.rule: All required features and interfaces are present and named.

risk.desc: Potential for thermal stress due to extreme temperature range.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management system and conduct thermal cycling tests.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fastener torque leading to joint failure.
risk.mit: Specify torque values and conduct torque verification tests.

risk.desc: Accessibility for maintenance and inspection may be compromised.
risk.mit: Ensure service access panels are easily removable and conduct human access tests.

risk.desc: Potential load transfer issues between wing spar and fuselage spine.
risk.mit: Conduct structural load tests to verify load transfer.

risk.desc: Risk of environmental damage due to lack of proper sealing.
risk.mit: Implement environmental barrier gaskets and conduct environmental tests.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

physics_paths.description: Rotor thrust and tilt servo loads are transferred through the wing spar into the fuselage spine.
Thermal energy from the wing skin is transferred to the spar and dissipated to the environment.
Access to the wing spar for maintenance and inspection.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Attach wing spar to fuselage spine

integration_opportunities.assembly_order_hint: Attach wing spar to fuselage spine

manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for FDM, toolpaths for CNC
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: CMM for dimensional accuracy, visual inspection for surface finish

verification_focus.description: Verify load transfer from wing spar to fuselage spine.
Verify thermal dissipation from wing skin to spar.
Verify accessibility for maintenance and inspection.

requirements: Subsystem module: Nacelle Assembly x 2 (fixed BOM item inside Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid)
Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

risk.desc: Potential thermal management issues due to insufficient heat dissipation paths.
risk.mit: Increase thermal duct size or add additional cooling channels.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly misalignment due to lack of primary datum feature.
risk.mit: Define and include a primary datum feature in the design.

risk.desc: Possible service access difficulties due to tight fit of access cover.
risk.mit: Ensure access cover has sufficient clearance for removal.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient load transfer from rotor to wing structure.
risk.mit: Review and reinforce load transfer path through tilt mechanism.

risk.desc: Potential mechanical failure due to inadequate servo linkage support.
risk.mit: Strengthen servo linkage mounting points and add reinforcement.

physics_paths.description: Rotor thrust is transferred through the tilt mechanism into the wing structure.
Servo-driven worm gear rotates the tilt axis.
Heat from the motor is dissipated to the environment through the nacelle.
ESC supplies power to the motor.
Service personnel can access the motor and ESC through the access cover.

integration_opportunities.description: Access cover allows for motor and ESC replacement.
Thermal duct provides a path for heat dissipation.
Servo linkage allows for tilt mechanism actuation.

verification_focus.description: Verify that rotor thrust is properly transferred to the wing structure.
Verify that motor heat is effectively dissipated.
Verify that the tilt mechanism operates smoothly.

risk.desc: Potential for thermal expansion to affect bearing seat alignment.
risk.mit: Implement thermal expansion gaps and use materials with similar thermal expansion coefficients.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for pushrod movement.

risk.desc: Risk of over-tightening bolts during assembly.

risk.desc: Risk of water ingress through service access panel.
risk.mit: Use gaskets and seals around service access panel.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling for servo in high load conditions.
risk.mit: Add more thermal dissipation fins or increase airflow around servo.

verify.rule: All required evidence_features are present in part_tree/geometry_ops.

risk.desc: Potential thermal hotspots around battery pack due to limited airflow.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the heat sink and optimize airflow paths.

risk.desc: Risk of cable strain if harness routing is not properly supported.
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief geometry and proper bend radius.

risk.desc: Risk of mis-mating connectors due to lack of keying or labeling.
risk.mit: Implement keying or labeling on connectors to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly misalignment if datum references are not controlled.
risk.mit: Define a primary datum feature and ensure assembly alignment is controlled.

risk.desc: Risk of improper torque application if torque values are not specified.
risk.mit: Specify torque values for all fasteners in the assembly sequence.
